reviews.review_description: This is a short story driven sci-fi adventure, I absolutely love it. However, I give it one star as it is unable to be downloaded at this time. Whether it be on the Go or GearVR.
The first gear vr game I have played where I completely lost track of time.  10 out of 5 stars!
But to short. I needed arround 40 minutes to finnish the game. Otherwise 5 stars. Pretty cool graphics for a Smartphone. Respect!
What's with it being advertised as designed for the gear vr controller?

played this on s7. Initially the sound effects were all off when I started the game. Turning on music, sfx, and ambience sound causes the game to lag immensely, it's not unplayable but still laggy enough to distract from the immersion. UPDATE: Turning ambience and controller off eliminates the lag. The music plays enough where the ambience isn't missed too much, great soundtrack though. the chapters play much quicker than lands end which is a similar game. the droid that follows you around tells you how to solve each puzzle. There really is no exploration, everything is spoonfed to you.... Each chapter is one basic puzzle all of which are incredibly easy, each level takes less than 10 minutes with only five levels. The game can be completed in less than an hour. I was hoping for more based on the trailer, the trailer basically shows you everything cool in the game. Not a lot of replayability either. Overall I'm disappointed in this game.

Nice gfx, music and most importantly an interesting world to navigate thought. Got it on sale and feel it was well worth that price. Not very long but I don't have enough time for long games so this was just right for me.
What a fantastic journey! Amazing in every way. Short, easy, yes indeed, but the point is to be relaxed, not stressed about solving tough puzzles. Absolutely worth  the money!

reviews.review_title: Could be better with two simple changes
reviews.review_description:
1) allow gaze selection to be sped up/overriden with an input event
2) allow direction/orientation changes without having to stand up or sit in a swivel chair.  Sometimes I just want to lean back into a couch, or in bed, or sit cross-legged somewhere, and if I can't play a game like that I will often just move on to something else
This is what I've waited for. Intense immersion. Varying movement. Interactive. And did I mention...gorgeous. these folks deserve recognition for this work. Thank you so much.

Let your little robot friend take you for a journey through a surreal space scape.

Slow paced and simple puzzles.

A fun with a unique style mixed with charm, pleasure and relax gameplay. Graphics in Althora is beautiful with nice landscape, great VR 3D effects. The story is interesting with plot twist. I recommend to play this game with headphone. The sound, music and ambient in this game is pretty good. This is a fantastic VR journey. A little short but you have a great time playing Althora.
This game is well made with smooth motion and detailed graphics, but... it's far to easy, it took me about 15 minutes to complete, so I don't feel its worth the price!
I loved it but I think it was a little short
Very nice. Short, especially for the price, but nice.
I guess my experience  with  Eclipse End of Light made me to hate this Althora. I wish I could get a refund

reviews.review_title: great environment, needs to be longer :)
reviews.review_description: i love the spaces and the world feel, and the scale... puzzles are too infrequent and too few... but good so far... sequel? let me wander the world?

I stopped playing as the immersion fades away when i realized that I'm a  giant person wearing a giant helmet talking to a robot that should be smaller in huge areas which are to giant!

Scale is totally off, which is disturbing. Try "lands end" or any other game; the relations in scale are very close to reallife. That's sad because I know i would like your game when the 3d scale is right. Please make an update or an option like in ansar wars where you can change/choose the 3d scale!
I notice the passionate work which went into development, therefore I don't understand the reason of the wrong scaling of the world and objects
The immersion is high on this one, so that's a plus. Gorgeous graphics and although the puzzles are kinda easy, I still highly recommend the game.
I was so excited for this game. But on the Oculus Go it was dropping frames and flickering a ton. I got sick to my stomach and could not finish. I wish I could get my money back.

Like color palette and environmental aspects. Was expecting longer play; 40 or less minutes start to finish. Not much in way of story and puzzles simple ( don’t care 4 hard, but these hardly even puzzles). Worth if priced under $1 but not more. Not bad, just needs price matched to comparative competition and content.
